# On Top of Pasketti: Children’s Speech Recognition Challenge Reference Implementation Repository

![Python 3.11](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11-blue) [![DrivenData Children's Speech Recognition Challenge](https://img.shields.io/badge/DrivenData-Children%E2%80%99s%20Speech%20Recognition%20Challenge-white?logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://kidsasr.drivendata.org/)


Welcome to the reference implementation repository for the [On Top of Pasketti: Children’s Speech Recognition Challenge](https://www.drivendata.org/competitions/group/childrens-asr-competition/)! In this challenge, there are two independent tracks: 

* In the Word Track, solvers will predict the words spoken by children in audio clips. 
* In the Phonetic Track, solvers will predict the speech sounds, or phones, spoken by children in audio clips. 

This repository contains a tutorial notebook for the Word Track and the Phonetic Track demonstrating how to:
 1. Load and explore competition data.
 2. Build a basic model.
 3. Package model for submission on the drivendata platform. 
 
 In the **[Word Track Reference Implementation Notebook](/notebooks/orthographic.ipynb)**, we show how to adapt NVIDIA's Parakeet, a pretrained automatic speech recognition ("ASR") model, using NeMo. In the **[Phonetic Track Reference Implementation Notebook](/notebooks/phonetic.ipynb)**, we show how to fine-tune Wav2Vec2, a pretrained speech representation model for ASR, using Hugging Face transformers. The notebooks are also available on the DrivenData blog (see the [word track reference implementation post](https://drivendata.co/blog/child-asr-word-benchmark) and the [phonetic track reference implementation post](https://drivendata.co/blog/child-asr-phonetic-reference-implementation)).

 All code necessary to replicate the tutorial notebooks is available in this repository. 
___

# Quick Start

Review the **[Word Track Reference Implementation Notebook](/notebooks/orthographic.ipynb)** and the **[Phonetic Track Reference Implementation Notebook](/notebooks/phonetic.ipynb)**. If you wish to execute these notebooks, instructions are provided below. 

## Create Virtual Environment

First, **create your environment**. To run the reference implementation tutorials, you must be on a Linux machine with GPU access. We use `uv` as the package manager in this repository.

1. Create an environment: `just create-environment`
2. Activate the environment: `source ./.venv/bin/activate`
3. Install the requirements found in the [TOML file](/pyproject.toml) into the environment: `just requirements`

*Note: You may need to update requirements to be compatible with your operating system.*

## Download Data

Next, **download competition data** to a `/data/raw` folder, and then unzip files. 

See the [Word Track Data Download](https://www.drivendata.org/competitions/308/childrens-word-asr/data/) page and the [Phonetic Track Data Download](https://www.drivendata.org/competitions/309/childrens-phonetic-asr/data/) page for access instructions. See the tutorial notebooks for more guidance on the expected data structure.

## Model Training and Submission

After creating your environment and downloading the data, **execute the tutorial notebook** to train a model.

### Orthographic 

Train the orthographic model by running the notebook as a script:
```bash
just train-orthographic
```
This executes notebooks/orthographic.ipynb as a script using `papermill`.

Once training is complete, package your submission for the platform:
```bash
just pack-orthographic
```
This command automatically finds the latest checkpoint, creates a symbolic link to ASR-Adapter-best.nemo, and bundles it with `main.py` into `orthographic_submission.zip`.

Before submitting, test your packaged model locally on sample data:
```bash
just test-orthographic
```
This runs `orthographic_submission/main.py` with the trained adapter checkpoint, performing inference on `data-demo` and writing results to `submission/submission.jsonl`.

### Phonetic

Train the phonetic model by running the notebook as a script:
```bash
just train-phonetic
```
This executes notebooks/phonetic.ipynb as a script using `papermill`.

Before submitting, test your model locally on sample data:
```bash
just test-phonetic
```
This runs `phonetic_submission/main.py` with `models/wav2vec2-phonetic-final/`, performing inference on `data-demo` and writing results to `submission/submission.jsonl`.

Package your submission for the platform:
```bash
just pack-phonetic
```
This command bundles `phonetic_submission/main.py` with `models/wav2vec2-phonetic-final/` into `phonetic_submission.zip`.

## Just commands

Useful, common commands are documented in a [justfile](https://just.systems/man/en/). To see all available commands, run `just --list`: 

```
just --list
Available recipes:
    clean                     # Delete all compiled Python files
    create-environment        # Set up Python interpreter environment
    default                   # List available commands (default when you run `just`)
    format                    # Format source code with ruff
    lint                      # Lint using ruff (use `just format` to do formatting)
    pack-orthographic         # Create zip for orthographic submission to challenge
    pack-phonetic             # Create zip for phonetic submission to challenge
    requirements              # Install Python dependencies
    test-orthographic         # Run inference using data-demo/word/ to test orthographic submission
    test-phonetic             # Run inference using data-demo/phonetic/ to test phonetic submission
    train-orthographic        # Run notebooks/orthographic.ipynb to train the orthographic NeMo adapter model
    train-phonetic            # Run notebooks/phonetic.ipynb to train the phonetic Wav2Vec2 model
```

## Project Organization

```
├── LICENSE                     <- Open-source license for this repository.
├── README.md                   <- You are here!
├── asr_benchmark/              <- Core Python package used by training, scoring, and submission code.
│   ├── __init__.py             <- Package marker and shared exports.
│   ├── assets/
│   │   └── asr_adaptation.yaml <- NeMo adapter/training configuration template.
│   ├── config.py               <- Central config helpers and path/constants used across workflows.
│   ├── nemo_adapter.py         <- NeMo adapter training/inference utilities.
│   └── score.py                <- Word Error Rate (WER) scoring utilities.
├── data/                       <- Local training/eval data root.
├── data-demo/                  <- Small demo dataset for quick smoke tests and local development.
├── justfile                    <- `just` task runner commands (train, test, package, sync, lint).
├── models/                     <- Trained model artifacts and related checkpoints.
├── notebooks/                  <- Reference implementation notebooks.
│   ├── images/                 
│   ├── orthographic.ipynb      <- Orthographic model training reference implementation.
│   └── phonetic.ipynb          <- Phonetic model training reference implementation.
├── orthographic_submission/
│   └── main.py                 <- Script to run inference for orthographic predictions.
├── phonetic_submission/
│   └── main.py                 <- Script to run inference for phonetic predictions.
├── pyproject.toml              <- Python project metadata and tool configuration.
└── uv.lock                     <- Locked dependency versions for reproducible environments.
```
